paper_title: Sparse Multi-Modal Transformer with Masking for Alzheimer's Disease Classification

problem_statement.challenge_1: Limited size of high-quality medical datasets, especially for early-stage AD and MCI.
problem_statement.challenge_2: Clinical data is heterogeneous and frequently incomplete, with missing modalities being common.

baseline_model.name: Cascaded Multi-Modal Mixing Transformer (3MT)
limitations: Computational complexity scales quadratically with sequence length (𝒪(n²)).
Lacks explicit mechanisms to address missing modalities.

proposed_model.name: Sparse Multi-Modal Transformer with Masking (SMMT)
innovations: A cluster-based sparse attention mechanism to reduce computational cost.
A modality-wise masking strategy to improve generalization by simulating missing modalities.

results.energy_efficiency_improvement: 40.4% reduction in training energy consumption compared to 3MT.

related_work_summary: Early approaches for AD diagnosis relied on single-modality data (e.g., structural MRI) using CNNs or support vector machines. Recent advances use deep learning for multi-modal data.
title: Sparse Multi-Modal Transformer with Masking for Alzheimer's Disease Classification
